results.title: Williams Companies AI Adoption Tracker | Larridin | AI Initiative Tracker
highlights:
Williams Companies has positioned itself strategically at the intersection of energy infrastructure and the AI revolution, leveraging its natural gas pipeline network to meet surging data center power demand [1]. The company is pursuing a comprehensive 'Power Innovation' strategy, investing over $5 billion in gas-fired power generation projects specifically designed to serve data centers and AI facilities [2]. Williams has also implemented AI internally through partnerships with technology firms like 27Global and Databricks, developing agentic chatbots for field operations and natural language analytics platforms that have reduced analyst backlogs by 90% [3]. CEO Chad Zamarin has positioned natural gas as 'America's affordability superpower' for powering the next generation of AI technology, emphasizing Williams' unique ability to provide speed-to-market power solutions through its extensive pipeline infrastructure [4].
...

results.title: Electrical Engineer III​/Senior-Power Innovation Project Development job in Oklahoma City, OK, United States at Williams
highlights:
Electrical Engineer III​/Senior-Power Innovation Project Development job in Oklahoma City, OK, United States at Williams
...
# Electrical Engineer III​/Senior-Power Innovation Project Development
...
Williams8 days ago
...
Oklahoma City, OK, United States
...
## Job Description
...
As an Electrical Engineer, you’ll lead the design and optimization of electrical systems that power essential infrastructure—pipelines, compressor stations, and storage facilities. You’ll apply your technical expertise to integrate advanced technologies like automation, real‑time monitoring, and energy storage, driving efficiency and innovation across operations. A key part of your role is ensuring safety through the implementation of arc flash mitigation, fault detection systems, and rigorous compliance with regulatory and environmental standards.
...
## Responsibilities
...
- Lead the design, implementation, and optimization of electrical systems and power distribution networks supporting critical infrastructure such as pipelines, compressor stations, and storage facilities.
...
- Champion the integration of advanced technologies, including automation, real‑time monitoring, and energy storage to enhance system performance and reduce operational costs.
...
- Implement robust safety measures such as arc flash mitigation and fault detection systems to protect personnel and ensure infrastructure reliability.
...
- Provide technical expertise in power distribution, electrical schematics, and system layouts for both new construction and upgrades to existing facilities.
...
- Manage complex engineering projects from concept to completion, ensuring alignment with business goals, timelines, and budget constraints.
...
- Ensure compliance with all relevant safety, environmental, and regulatory standards across electrical systems and project execution.
...
## Education and Experience
...
- Senior Level: Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical Engineering from an ABET‑accredited university, OR Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering Technology from an ABET‑accredited university with a Professional Engineer (P.E.) license and eight (8) years of engineering experience in natural gas, data centers, or related industry. Preferred: P.E. license. Preferred: Master’s degree in electrical engineering.
...
- III Level: Bachelor’s Degree in Electrical Engineering from an ABET‑accredited university, OR Bachelor’s Degree in Engineering Technology from an ABET‑accredited university with a Professional Engineer (P.E.) license and four (4) years of engineering experience in natural gas, data centers, or related industry. Preferred: P.E. license. Preferred: Master’s degree in electrical engineering.
...
## Job Requirements
...
- Demonstrates excellent organizational and interpersonal skills with safety as a main priority.
- Proficiency in Microsoft Office applications and PC skills.
- Proficiency in reviewing arc flash studies and implementing improvements.
- Working knowledge of electrical design codes (NEC, NEMA, API, IEEE, etc.).
- Preferred: Experience with PLCs.
- Preferred: Project Management experience.
- Preferred: Experience working in Preliminary‑Front End Engineering and Design (Pre‑FEED), Front End Engineering Design (FEED), or Engineering, Procurement, Construction (EPC) of small to medium facility projects.
- Preferred: Proficiency in designing and implementing instrumentation systems, including Programmable Logic Controllers (PLC) and Human Machine Interface (HMI) systems.
...
## Required Skills
...
Power Distribution

PLC and HMI Systems Design
...
Automation Technologies

Project Management

Arc Flash Mitigation
...
Electrical Systems Design

Electrical Design Codes (NEC, NEMA, API, IEEE)
...
Real-time Monitoring
...
Energy Storage Systems

Fault Detection Systems

results.title: How AI helped Williams cut field work from days to hours and speed software delivery  | Williams Companies
highlights:
How AI helped Williams cut field work from days to hours and speed software delivery | Williams Companies
...
In day-to-day operations, minutes matter, and so does the time it takes to prepare work safely and consistently. At a compressor station in Virginia, a preventive maintenance checklist for electrical equipment that could take a full day, sometimes two, was completed in about 1.5 hours using approved AI tools and refined prompts.
...
At Williams, AI is used to speed up repeatable tasks and improve access to reliable information, helping teams spend less time assembling documents and more time planning, prioritizing and executing safely. The goal is practical: reduce administrative drag, increase consistency and support better decisions across the business.
...
’ approach is governed and people-led: teams use approved tools, and employees remain
...
for the decisions and actions those tools
...
By putting tools such as Microsoft Copilot in the hands of frontline gathering and processing teams, Williams is shortening the time from idea to execution, helping teams get more value from the data they already have, such as spotting patterns that contribute to downtime.
...
The same focus on cycle time is showing up in software delivery. Williams’ IT teams are using AI-assisted coding to modernize applications and deliver new software faster while maintaining strong cost discipline. In one modernization example, AI-enabled development reduced implementation cost by 55% and delivered 60% faster.
...
What this means: Copilot helps teams automate routine work like preventive maintenance checklists and troubleshooting guides, enabling faster issue resolution and more consistent execution.
...
Q: What AI tools are employees using at Williams?
...
A: Employees are using Microsoft Copilot and other approved function-specific AI tools as part of daily work.
...
Q: What business results has AI supported so
...
A: Commercial teams improved how they use storage data, generating a $7 million uplift in “park and loan” business (a type of natural gas storage transaction) and reducing rework by bringing key operational and commercial information into a single, real-time view.
...
Q: How is AI helping frontline operations teams and IT delivery?
...
A: Frontline teams use AI to automate routine work such as preventive maintenance lists and troubleshooting guides; in one Virginia compressor station example, a list that used to take a full day, sometimes two, was completed in about 1.5 hours. Williams’ IT teams are also using AI-assisted coding to modernize applications, with one example reducing implementation costs by 55% and delivering 60% faster.

results.title: AI helps F&A teams reduce manual work, move faster | Williams Companies
highlights:
AI helps F&A teams reduce manual work, move faster | Williams Companies
...
- Teams are using AI to reduce manual tasks and support faster, more informed decision-making.
- Employee-led experimentation and idea sharing are helping identify practical uses across Williams.
- The focus is on applying AI thoughtfully, simplifying work first and automating where it adds value.
...
Artificial intelligence is reshaping how Williams’ Finance and Accounting teams approach their work. Across the organization, employees are using AI to speed analysis, improve consistency and free up more time for higher-value decision-making.
...
The result is not just faster work for one team, but a growing example of how employees across Williams can use AI to solve everyday business challenges.
...
There’s no single way we’re using AI. The applications are broad, and that’s what makes AI so powerful. Teams are finding different ways to strengthen their work.
...
Employee-led experimentation is a key part of that momentum, said Mary Hausman, vice president and chief accounting officer.
...
The people doing the work are the ones finding
...
Faster analysis with Copilot
...
In reporting, AI is helping teams get to insights faster.
...
Accounting teams are using Copilot to reduce the effort required to produce variance analyses, cutting time spent gathering information and giving employees more time to focus on what the numbers mean.
...
Instead of building every analysis manually, teams can move more quickly from data gathering to insight.
...
Employees are also using AI to build tools that can scale across teams.
...
One example is an AI-built asset cheat sheet now used across the company to help employees understand our assets. Accounting supervisor Eugene McLaughlin created the resource with AI, turning it into a practical tool employees can use to quickly find information about major facilities and systems.
...
Hausman said Williams has built an innovation network across teams where employees are sharing ideas, bringing forward challenges and working together on solutions.
...
As more employees see AI in action, interest continues to grow and teams are spotting new opportunities to improve workflows. At the same time, she said the goal is not to apply technology for its own sake.
...
“Sometimes it’s about stepping back, asking why and simplifying the work before you automate it,” Hausman said.
...
“Once people see real examples, it creates excitement,” she added. “It’s no longer theoretical. They can see how it works and start coming up with their own ideas.”

results.title: How shared, real-time storage data helped generate $7 million in uplift | Williams Companies
highlights:
How shared, real-time storage data helped generate $7 million in uplift | Williams Companies
...
When commercial and operations teams can see the same constraints at the same time, decisions get faster and results can follow. At Williams, improvements to how teams use storage data generated a $7 million uplift in “park and loan” activity, a type of storage transaction.
...
Williams uses AI as a practical tool to reduce routine work and surface clearer operational context, so teams can make decisions faster and focus their time on higher-value, judgment-heavy work. The company emphasizes responsible use: AI supports the work, while employees remain accountable for outcomes.
...
“The payoff was faster alignment and a repeatable way to turn shared data into measurable value,” said Irma Glover, manager of commercial technology. What this means: Shared, real-time data helps teams align faster and deliver business outcomes, improving how work gets done across commercial and operational groups while keeping accountability with the people closest to the decisions.
...
Read more about how Williams is using AI.

results.title: Williams Companies (Power Innovation) - The Infinite Unknown
highlights:
Williams Companies is a Tulsa, Oklahoma-based natural gas pipeline and processing operator (NYSE: WMB) that launched a Power Innovation program to build dedicated behind-the-meter gas turbine power plants for AI data centers. Williams leverages its existing pipeline infrastructure to deliver gas supply and build generation capacity directly adjacent to hyperscale campuses. Its first project — “Socrates” in New Albany, Ohio — is a 400 MW off-grid plant serving Meta, scheduled to enter service late 2026.
...
- Founded: 1908 (Tulsa, OK); NYSE: WMB
- Type: Publicly traded pipeline operator / BTM power developer
- Status: Active — Power Innovation program underway; Socrates (Ohio) under construction as of 2026
- Capital commitment: $5.1 billion committed to Power Innovation buildout
- Target capacity: 6+ GW of BTM power across Texas, Tennessee, and Ohio by 2027
- First project: Socrates — two × 200 MW gas plants at New Albany, Ohio for Meta (10-year agreement)
- Turbine OEMs used: Solar Turbines (Titan 250, PGM 130), Siemens Energy (SGT-400), Caterpillar (3520 engines)
...
Williams Companies built its business on natural gas transmission — its Transco pipeline is the largest-volume natural gas pipeline in the US. The Power Innovation program extends that vertical by having Williams build, own, and operate gas-fired generation assets that sit physically adjacent to data center campuses and supply power directly, bypassing the electric grid entirely.
...
are designed to operate entirely off-grid: “will operate
...
-the-
...
the electric load of
...
and will not be physically connected to the
...
power grid,”
...
Ohio regulatory filings. This
...
avoids grid interconnection review, M
...
- 2026-Q3 (projected): Socrates power plants (New Albany, Ohio) scheduled to enter service — 400 MW total, serving Meta’s Prometheus campus and adjacent rapid deployment structures
- 2026-Q1: Williams reported expanding Power Innovation pipeline; 6+ GW target across Texas, Tennessee, Ohio by 2027; $5.1B committed capital; Q1 2026 earnings showed strong data center pipeline growth
- 2026-Mar: Williams published explainer on BTM power model for data centers, signaling intent to market the model broadly — Williams.com
...
- ~2025-Q3: Broke ground on Socrates (Ohio) project following Ohio Public Utilities Commission approval; two-plant design using modular gas units
- 2025: Ohio regulators approved construction of 200 MW gas plant (first Socrates unit) to serve Meta New Albany campus — Data Center Dynamics
- 2024–2025: Williams announced Power Innovation program at CERAWeek 2024; initial design centered on modular gas units (Solar Turbines, Siemens, Caterpillar) for fast deployment in power-constrained markets
...
Research on Williams Power Innovation leadership is ongoing. Williams Companies CEO is Alan Armstrong (long tenure; known for pipeline growth strategy). Power Innovation division leadership not yet identified in public sources.
...

results.title: Williams' Bold Move: Securing AI Energy Supply with Gas Assets (2026)
highlights:
Williams' Bold Move: Securing AI Energy Supply with Gas Assets (2026)
...
# Williams' Bold Move: Securing AI Energy Supply with Gas Assets (2026)
...
The energy industry is on the brink of a controversial shift, with a potential move that could reshape the market. Sources reveal that Williams, a prominent energy infrastructure operator, is considering a bold step into natural gas production, a move that could significantly impact the AI energy supply chain.
...
But here's the twist: Williams isn't just aiming to produce natural gas; it's eyeing a strategic acquisition of gas-producing assets to enhance its position in the market. This move is part of a larger strategy to become a one-stop energy shop for hyperscalers and data centers, a growing sector with massive energy demands.
...
The company has already invested over $5 billion in power projects for digital infrastructure, and now it's looking to expand its upstream assets. By owning these assets, Williams could position itself as the sole energy partner for hyperscalers, eliminating the need for multiple negotiations and gaining a competitive edge.
...
However, this strategy is not without risks. Sources close to the matter warn that the plan is not set in stone, and the company remains tight-lipped about its future moves. Williams' statement hints at a potential shift, stating they evaluate opportunities to advance their natural gas-focused strategy, but they remain cautious about revealing more.
...
And here's where it gets intriguing: As hyperscalers and AI developers face the challenge of securing consistent power, Williams' power generation projects become crucial. The Socrates project in Ohio, backed by Meta Platforms, and the upcoming Apollo and Aquila projects, are set to provide substantial power. These additions to Williams' infrastructure, including its extensive pipeline network, could significantly boost earnings, with analysts eagerly awaiting the company's earnings report and analyst day.
...
Historically, U.S. oil and gas companies favored an integrated model, owning a mix of production, storage, transportation, and refining assets. However, the industry's shift towards specialization led many companies to divest non-core assets. Williams itself spun off its upstream business, WPX Energy, which later merged with Devon Energy.
...
As Williams considers this strategic acquisition, the energy industry watches with anticipation. Will this move pay off, or will it face unexpected challenges? The debate is open, and the impact on the market could be significant. Stay tuned as this story unfolds, and feel free to share your thoughts on this potential game-changer in the energy sector.

This position is posted by Jobgether on behalf of a partner company. We are currently looking for an AI / ML Engineer in the United States.This role sits at the intersection of machine learning engineering, AI systems design, and large-scale data-driven decision-making, focused on solving complex challenges in the energy and utility sector. You will contribute to the development of advanced AI and ML solutions that combine predictive modeling, optimization, and generative AI to support critical infrastructure and sustainability goals. Working closely with data scientists and software engineers, you will help design, build, and deploy scalable machine learning systems that power real-world applications. The environment is highly collaborative and innovation-driven, with a strong emphasis on applied AI, production readiness, and measurable impact. You will work across diverse use cases including forecasting, geospatial analysis, and intelligent decision systems. This is a high-impact role where your work directly supports the modernization and efficiency of energy systems.Accountabilities
